intro.uz: Soʻnggi dars — Seth Godinʼning "Tribes" gʻoyasi haqida: qabila endi oila emas, balki bir maʼnoni va maqsadni baham koʻradigan odamlar toʻdasi. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasi aynan odamlar va ravonlik haqida: ergash gaplar yakuni (nodefining, qisqargan va takror) va urgʻu-ravonlik yakuni (cleft "What…is…" / "It's…that…" hamda nutq bogʻlovchilari va yumshatish). Tony haqidagi POV hikoyada bularning zaminini eshitasiz.
intro.en: The final lesson is about Seth Godin's idea of "Tribes": a tribe is no longer just a family but a group of people who share a meaning and a purpose. This week's two grammar topics are all about people and fluency: the relative-clause capstone (non-defining, reduced and review) and the emphasis-and-fluency capstone (cleft "What…is…" / "It's…that…" plus discourse markers and hedging). You'll hear the groundwork in the POV story about Tony.

canDo.uz: Dars oxirida oʻz "qabilangiz"dagi odamlarni ergash gaplar bilan (vergulli qoʻshimcha va qisqargan shakllar) toʻxtamasdan tasvirlab bera olasiz va fikringizni cleft (What…is… / It's…that…) hamda yumshatuvchi iboralar bilan ravon, urgʻu bilan ayta olasiz.
canDo.en: By the end you can describe the people in your "tribe" with relative clauses (non-defining and reduced) without pausing, and give your point with emphasis and fluency using cleft sentences (What…is… / It's…that…) and hedging phrases.

grammar.titleUz: Ergash gaplar yakuni: qoʻshimcha (nodefining), qisqargan va takror
grammar.titleEn: Relative clauses capstone — non-defining, reduced & review
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS Part 2 — describe a person and add extra detail smoothly
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can add extra information about people with non-defining and reduced relative clauses
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida sifatlovchi ergash gap ismdan OLDIN keladi: "menga ishongan ustozim". Ingliz tilida esa KEYIN: "my mentor who believed in me". Yana bir farq — oʻzbekchada vergul bilan aniqlovchi/qoʻshimchani ajratmaymiz, ingliz tilida esa aynan vergul maʼnoni oʻzgartiradi: "My brother who lives in London" (bir nechta aka) ≠ "My brother, who lives in London," (aka bitta).
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ My mentor, that believed in me, changed my life. ✅ My mentor, who believed in me, changed my life. Vergulli (qoʻshimcha) ergash gapda "that" ishlatilmaydi — faqat who/which. "that" faqat vergulsiz aniqlovchi gaplarga xos.

examples.en: My mentor, who believed in me, changed my life.
examples.uz: Ustozim, menga ishongan boʻlgani uchun, hayotimni oʻzgartirdi.

examples.en: Seth Godin, who wrote "Tribes", is a business writer.
examples.uz: "Tribes" kitobini yozgan Seth Godin — biznes yozuvchisi.

examples.en: A real tribe is a group grounded in respect.

exercises.prompt: Seth Godin, ___ wrote "Tribes", is a business writer.
exercises.hintUz: vergulli (qoʻshimcha) gap + odam → who; "that" bu yerda ishlatilmaydi

exercises.prompt: Rwanda, ___ is in Africa, needed real help.
The people ___ me are all volunteers. (who are helping → qisqargan)

exercises.promptUz: Oʻz "qabilangiz"dagi bir odamni tasvirlang (ovoz chiqarib): "My …, who …, …" — vergulli qoʻshimcha ergash gap bilan.
exercises.hintUz: ism + vergul + who/which + qoʻshimcha maʼlumot + vergul

grammar.titleUz: Urgʻu va ravonlik yakuni: cleft (What…is… / It's…that…) + nutq bogʻlovchilari
grammar.titleEn: Emphasis & fluency capstone — cleft + discourse markers
grammar.bandLifter: IELTS — the Fluency & Coherence lifter: emphasise a point and hedge naturally
grammar.cefrCanDo: B2: I can emphasise a key idea with cleft sentences and soften my speech with hedging
grammar.contrastUz: Oʻzbek tilida urgʻuni soʻz tartibi yoki "aynan" soʻzi beradi: "Seni aynan mana shu odamlar ushlab turadi". Ingliz tili buni maxsus qolip bilan quradi: "It's these people that keep you going". Yumshatishda ham farq bor: oʻzbekcha "-ku, -da, aslida"; inglizcha esa I'd say, kind of, in a way kabi alohida iboralar bilan yumshatadi.
grammar.errorFixUz: ❌ What a tribe gives you are belonging. It's the people which keep you going. ✅ What a tribe gives you IS belonging. It's the people WHO keep you going. What-cleftdan keyin feʼl bitta narsaga moslanadi (is), odamlar uchun esa it-cleftda who ishlatiladi.

examples.en: What a tribe gives you is belonging.
examples.uz: Qabila senga beradigan narsa — bu tegishlilik hissi.

examples.en: It's the people who keep you going.
To be honest, it was love that Tony needed, not power.
examples.uz: Rostini aytsam, Tonyga kerak boʻlgan narsa — kuch emas, sevgi edi.

exercises.prompt: ___ a tribe gives you is belonging.
exercises.hintUz: What-cleft: What + gap + is + urgʻuli qism

exercises.prompt: It's the people ___ keep you going.
___, a tribe is really about belonging. (yumshatish)
exercises.hintUz: yumshatuvchi/bogʻlovchi ibora jumla boshida: To be honest…

exercises.promptUz: Bitta muhim fikringizni cleft bilan ayting (ovoz chiqarib): "What matters most to me is …" yoki "It's … that …", boshiga "I'd say…" qoʻshib.
exercises.hintUz: What…is… yoki It's…that… + yumshatuvchi ibora (I'd say / to be honest)

vocab.uz: qabila; bir maʼno/maqsadni baham koʻradigan odamlar guruhi
vocab.defEn: a group of people who share a meaning, purpose or interest
vocab.example: The Effortless English Club is really a tribe.

vocab.defEn: to want or desire something very strongly
vocab.example: Every tribe yearns for leadership and connection.

vocab.defEn: to put separate parts together to make a whole
vocab.example: You have a chance to assemble a tribe and lead it.

vocab.defEn: increased power or a way to get more influence
vocab.example: The Internet gives individuals more leverage.

vocab.uz: qiziqish doiralari (bir narsani yoqtiradigan kishilar guruhi)
vocab.defEn: groups of people who share the same interest or hobby
vocab.example: A fan club is a circle of interest, not quite a tribe.

vocab.defEn: to improve a situation or someone's life; to help
vocab.example: Anyone who wants to make a difference can.

vocab.defEn: strong confidence and belief in something
vocab.example: Tribes are about faith and belief in an idea.

vocab.defEn: connected to and starting from something
vocab.example: A real tribe is grounded in respect and admiration.

vocab.defEn: a shared aim that connects people
vocab.example: A modern tribe is connected by a common purpose.
Belief in a tribe is a brilliant strategy.

warmup.uz: Bu suhbatda ikki doʻst oilaviy yigʻin haqida gaplashadi va turli qarindoshlarni sanaydi: brother-in-law, great uncle, step-son, ex-wife… Qabila (tribe) darsiga mos: oila — eng birinchi "qabilamiz". Sizningcha, ingliz tilidagi qarindosh nomlari oʻzbekchadan koʻra aniqroqmi yoki chalkashroqmi?
warmup.en: In this dialogue two friends talk about a family get-together and list all kinds of relatives: brother-in-law, great uncle, step-son, ex-wife… It fits the Tribes theme: family is our very first "tribe". Do you think English relative names are clearer or more confusing than Uzbek ones?

keyVocab.uz: qaynaka/kuyov (turmush oʻrtogʻining akasi yoki singlisining eri)
keyVocab.defEn: the husband of your sister, or the brother of your spouse
the son or daughter of an uncle or aunt
the sister of one's father or mother
the brother of one's grandmother or grandfather
a son of one's husband or wife from a previous marriage
a woman who was formerly a particular man's wife

quiz.q: What's the name for the study of ancient handwriting?
quiz.qUz: Qadimgi qoʻlyozmalarni oʻrganadigan fan qanday ataladi?
quiz.explanationUz: Toʻgʻri javob: b) Palaeography. Neil a) Scriptography dedi va xato qildi. Palaeography — qadimgi va tarixiy qoʻlyozmalarni oʻqish va sanasini aniqlash bilan shugʻullanadigan fan.

vocab.defEn: your origins; where your family comes from
embarrassing secrets that are kept hidden
an official count of the people in a population
knowledge and stories passed on from one generation to the next
connected by a blood relationship to an ancestor

funEnglish.title: The tribes we lead - Seth Godin

speakingPrompt.en: Tell me about your "tribe" — the people who keep you going. Who is in it, and what is it that really connects you?
speakingPrompt.uz: Taxminan 60 soniya gapiring. Bu haftaning ikki grammatikasidan foydalaning: ergash gaplar (My friend, who…; the people helping me…) va cleft/urgʻu (What connects us is…; It's … that …), yumshatuvchi iboralar bilan (I'd say…, to be honest…).
